description: Witness manifest — the conceptual mirror of seed-ladder.cmd. The ingestion order is a SIGNAL-DEPENDENCY STACK, not a skip list: each layer is a different kind of signal that presupposes the prior. English-scoped by default (LAPLACE_INGEST_LANGS=en); the decomposition logic is language-agnostic, so flipping the filter to all languages runs the same code.

language_scope.law: Every witness source remains in the manifest. Language filters reduce which languages are ingested from multilingual corpora — not which witnesses exist. DecomposerOptions.ForWitness falls back to LanguageFilter.ForSource(sourceKey), which reads LAPLACE_INGEST_LANGS — so the env scopes every decomposer uniformly; none needs a bespoke --langs flag.

cadence_law: floor (atoms + the language axis) → document (raw-text distributional trajectories: the corpus that proves AI-via-SQL answers from text ALONE) → knowledge (the *Net synset hub + the semantic/syntactic graph — uniform :ingest steps converging on shared content anchors) → usage (language in use) → code capstone (the full tree-sitter/grammar/AST pipeline; the precondition for model export) → models (LAST, because deposition presupposes export). Each layer ENRICHES the prior; none is 'deferred bulk'. The QKVO/FFN-exact analog lives in the knowledge layer; the de-blob lives there.
anchor_law: Concepts are decomposed CONTENT, never OfCanonical("witness/namespace/string") blobs. A synset's identity is its Collaborative Interlingual Index (CILI, e.g. i46531) decomposed to codepoints — NOT the WordNet byte-offset, which is a file address that differs across languages and versions. Witness → source_id; category (synset/sense/class/frame/roleset) and pos → IS_A / HAS_POS attestations. Every language's distinct wordforms (dog != perro — never merged) converge by attesting onto the same shared concept anchor. The codepoint floor is the only dedup level. The shared de-blob surfaces are ConceptAnchor (ILI-resolving) and CategoryAnchor (key-as-content + IS_A); decomposers are thin callers, never minting their own blobs.

sources.role: codepoint atoms — the only dedup floor
the language axis; 639-1/-2/-3 codes for one language converge to one language entity, macrolanguage = IS_A/HAS_MEMBER

cadence.law: RIGHT AFTER the floor. Distributional PRECEDES/FOLLOWS/CO_OCCURS_WITH trajectories — the exact signal a transformer trains on. 'You can ingest unicode, iso, and just the books and start answering questions': attention needs nothing more to start. Everything below ENRICHES; it is not the core.

sources.role: books / long text (mobydick, dracula, tale, ...) — raw-text corpus that proves AI-via-SQL

cadence.law: The *Net synset hub then the semantic/syntactic graph — UNIFORM :ingest steps (no source special-cased). wordnet first: the rest bind to its ILI-anchored synsets/senses. Attested typed structure (IS_A, hypernymy, synonymy, roles, frames, commonsense, syntax, omniglottal breadth).

sources.links: synsets (ILI-anchored content) + senses + lemma arena + pointer graph — THE anchor. Offset resolves to ILI via CILI; members IS_SYNONYM_OF synset, gloss HAS_DEFINITION, pointers typed relations.
expand method: per-language wordforms attest onto the SAME ILI synset — no cross-language wordform dedup. RAW ss_type (satellite s != head a).
Levin classes (CategoryAnchor); members carry wn sense keys → WN synset → ILI; thematic roles → shared role inventory.
rolesets (CategoryAnchor) → VerbNet class → WN → ILI; args Arg0-4 + ArgM standardized.
frames + FEs + LUs (CategoryAnchor); LUs map to WN synsets → ILI.
PB<->VN<->FN alignment, CORRESPONDS_TO only (owners attest IS_A). Predicate Matrix extends the bridge to WN (WSD-aligned) → ILI.
/c/<lang>/word[/pos/wn/sense] graph — word=content, lang=attestation, /r/X relations universal across languages; /wn/ carries the synset→ILI bridge; TranslationOf cross-lingual links.
event heads ('PersonX gives Y') = event anchors; xIntent/xEffect/... role-typed; converges with ConceptNet via RelationTypeRegistry.
forms/lemmas=content, UPOS→PosReference, deprel→core+subtype FEATURE (ResolveDeprel), features→ResolveFeature — all universal/omniglottal.
wordforms=content, lang=attestation, senses.glosses=HAS_DEFINITION, translations=cross-lingual concept links (wiktextract jsonl).

cadence.law: Language in use: cross-lingual alignment + dialogue. Part of the cadence — SKIP_USAGE=1 is an iteration knob, not a 'deferred' verdict.

sources.role: sentences = content trajectories; links.csv → IS_TRANSLATION_OF (cross-lingual alignment)

cadence.law: Boil the ocean, RIGHT BEFORE models: all code info together through the full tree-sitter/grammar/AST pipeline. Success = the whole machinery works end-to-end — the precondition for model export. X_BONEYARD/incidental local repos are NOT the default capstone.

sources.role: code-reasoning snippets — lives in D:/Models/hub (HF), not D:/Data/Ingest

cadence.law: LAST — model ingestion presupposes that model EXPORT works (the foundry is ingest's inverse), which the code capstone proves the machinery for. UNSET/0 = models REQUIRED (error if missing).

stop_and_resume_knobs.LAPLACE_LADDER_START: floor (default) | proof — proof skips floor + knowledge for a resume.
stop_and_resume_knobs.LAPLACE_LADDER_STOP: nets = stop after the knowledge layer; usage = stop after usage. The scoped iteration targets for the lexical/semantic decomposers — NEVER a substitute for the full-cadence gate.
stop_and_resume_knobs.LAPLACE_LADDER_DRY: 1 = print the resolved plan, ingest nothing.
stop_and_resume_knobs.LAPLACE_SKIP_MODELS: 1 skips safetensor deposits; UNSET/0 = models REQUIRED.

authority_sources.law: Official language/platform sources and their documentation are the highest-trust code witnesses: the language's own repository testifies its idioms; its docs are the Rosetta intent<->implementation corpus. Acquire with download-code-data.cmd authority; ingest via the repo loop inside the code capstone.

clones.witnesses: C + SQL idioms from the database the substrate lives in
C + Python from the language's source of truth
learn.microsoft.com content as markdown — intent<->code pairs
C# + C from the platform's source of truth (largest — ingest last)

test_data.law: test-data/text is ingested via 'ingest document' in the document stage. db-roundtrip on the same files is proof-only (ingest-text.cmd / e2e-full.cmd) — bit-perfect is for raw user content, NOT seed/model data (which is consensus-folded).
